question: Implement a `StringWrapper` class that manages a C-style string (`char*`) dynamically allocated on the heap. Provide a copy constructor that performs a deep copy to prevent dangling pointers.

test_cases.expected:
--- StringWrapper Test (Logical Error) ---
s1: StringWrapper content: "Short"
s2 (copy of s1): StringWrapper content: "Short"

--- Longer String Test ---
s3: StringWrapper content: "LongerStringExample"
s4 (copy of s3): StringWrapper content: "LongerStringExample"

--- End Test ---

errors: The copy constructor allocates `strlen(other.data)` bytes instead of `strlen(other.data) + 1`, leading to a buffer overflow when `strcpy` attempts to write the null terminator. This results in memory corruption or a crash.
